+pyramid

This item is called **Origin Of Time**, it provides two main benefits:

**Full Refund**: Recovers all XP and materials you invested in the character you use it on.

**Duplicate Conversion**: Only for Standard Awakeners (not for Limited in the Standard pool or the starting SR Awakeners), converts all additional copies into Standard "selectors," which can subsequently be used to obtain or duplicate other Standard Awakeners of your choice (not Limited in Standard).

This process is also applicable to **Standard Wheels** (except the Limited in the Standard pool).

It is primarily obtained from the developers through special in-game mail during specific events, such as anniversaries or major updates. It is used for re-allocating resources to build a better team.

Checklist for Arachne pursuit FAQ:
- Did you have at least 1 (one) stack of 'Weaver'?
> If not, your Arachne could not trigger pursuit due to not having stacks, similar to Vortice's pursuit. 'Weaver' can be acquired through playing Arachne's 1-cost, then the 0-cost card created from that.
- Were you in Ultra Round/Did you Annihilate?
> If not, then you failed to meet one of her pursuit requirements, please try again while within one of those two conditions
- Was her exalt used AFTER triggering Annihilation/entering Ultra Round?
> If you used your Exalt **before** triggering those, you also failed to meet the prerequisite condition for Arachne's pursuit, please try reversing the order of operations there 
- Did you play a DIFFERENT character's command card after **all of the above** were completed?
> If you only played Arachne's command cards after, then your pursuit wouldn't have triggered, considering she only pursuits if **another** character's command card is played, not her own
If you completed all of the above and Arachne's pursuit still failed to trigger, please file a complaint within [insert official Q&A channel here] and hope that the official devs will reply to you soon!

Checklist for Arachne pursuit FAQ:
- Did you have at least 1 (one) stack of 'Weaver'?
> If not, your Arachne could not trigger pursuit due to not having stacks, similar to Vortice's pursuit. 'Weaver' can be acquired through playing Arachne's 1-cost, then the 0-cost card created from that.
- Were you in Ultra Round/Did you Annihilate?
> If not, then you failed to meet one of her pursuit requirements, please try again while within one of those two conditions
- Was her exalt used AFTER triggering Annihilation/entering Ultra Round?
> If you used your Exalt **before** triggering those, you also failed to meet the prerequisite condition for Arachne's pursuit, please try reversing the order of operations there 
- Did you play a DIFFERENT character's command card after **all of the above** were completed?
> If you only played Arachne's command cards after, then your pursuit wouldn't have triggered, considering she only pursuits if **another** character's command card is played, not her own
If you completed all of the above and Arachne's pursuit still failed to trigger, please file a complaint within [insert official Q&A channel here] and hope that the official devs will reply to you soon!

Let me tell you about why max HP is important with this team:

Vortice's Aequor is not normal. It is called Divine Realm. If you were to read what it does, you will see that both your base tentacle damage *and* your white stance shield depend on your max HP (I hope you know how to switch stances

Caro is the Realm that cares the most about your max HP. Their 2 of their mechanics, Devour and Furnace, defensively scale on your max HP (both devour shield furnace in general)(I hope you know about Furnace)

Combine the two, as you have done in this team, and you now have a team that cares a LOT about your max HP.
In arc 2 rules, 75% of your DR is converted into up to an additional 10% more max HP. This means that in arc 2 getting DR gives you more HP, which with this team is very good.
So you can use Rabbit for the shielding, or use Burial for the little bit of max HP
This (the DR-HP conversion) only works on arc 2 rules however, (astral reign), so burial is not that useful in Arc 1 unless you plan to proc DR

After getting Goliath's weapon with 12 dupes, where should I spend my pulls? In the realm that has limited units that complement my teams, even if the probability is low?

Here's how I think of it:
Required=>
- At least 3 threads or its kind of pointless 
- I need to be in an ultra round or annihilation round
- I need to be able to exalt with Arachne.

Considerations
- I want Arith so I can trigger it enough times (ideally 5)
- I want to use cheapest card possible of other units to trigger max pursuits
- If possible, I can try to get 6+ pursuits if I play smart with Arachne cards

Notes:
- you can accumulate threads in previous fights. they persist between fights. always have 3+ before going into a boss
- arachne needs 150 to exalt, so its good to plan ahead and save up for ult rounds
- use annihilation round to delay ult rounds for bosses if possible, generally ult round will work best
- on bosses, burn non-pierce regular dmg cards first because in next phase theyll be immune to non-pierce, non-thread dmg
- pile up the pursuits after they are immune from boss rouse to clear 2 phases in 1 turn

if you're building around jenkin, earlygame she doesn't actually need much help, and is capable of one-shotting all non-boss enemies using her aoe 1-cost in ch 1&2, but characters she appreciates include winkle (aliemus/ultimate charge), arachne (shield & makes getting kills easier, though she's not a huge fan of s.ultra), and just whoever else you feel like bringing, preferably someone who doesn't want a lot of arithmetica and can defend reasonably alright
i personally used karen for her exalt (gives you a card that gives 3 arith), but the thing with jenkins is that as a standard crit dps, she can work with just about anyone who isn't detrimental

good options on banner rn do, as i said, include arachne, but also sometimes gdoll, for her general-use support capacity (though i wouldn't recommend going all in for her at the start, since she gets better in mid- to late-game when you're more familiar with mechanics
none of the current banners rn are her best teammate though, that being castor, who provides really nice big shields & corrosion, which can improve her dps ability a lot more
horla is also a character you'll want to keep an eye out for when she reruns (though she reran recently so i wouldn't count on it happening anytime soon)
